source_jobs.title: Project Architect
source_jobs.description:
The Role: Project Architect

We are hiring a Project Architect to lead project-level execution and permitting for a prefabricated housing platform, translating a standardized product system into approved, buildable projects across multiple jurisdictions.

This role involves site planning, permitting, coordination of architectural, structural, and MEP documentation at the project level.

You will ensure projects move efficiently through local jurisdictions without compromising the integrity of the product platform. This is a hands-on role. You are expected to produce drawings, coordinate consultants and with local jurisdictions, and resolve issues in real time.

The role is based full-time in Austin, TX and would be ideal for an experienced designer who prefers to work hands-on, with a potential to grow a team over time.

 

About The Company

The American Housing Corporation http://americanhousing.co is on a mission to solve the America’s housing crisis through all-out housing production. We believe that America’s affordability crisis is fundamentally a production crisis, and that solving it requires a vertically integrated development approach: We buy land, design and manufacture, install, and sell our rowhomes directly to customers — all by ourselves.

We're hiring the best engineers, designers, and builders to solve one of the hardest problem in America. We’re based in Austin, TX and build across the country.

 

What You’ll Be Working On

 - Site planning and feasibility: support Real Estate team with conducting site analysis and feasibility analysis, applying our proprietary prefabricated products and designs.

 - Project delivery: lead project execution from concept to entitlement, permitting, and construction support.

 - Documentation and coordination: develop complete permitting and construction documentation; coordinate with local consultants on site specific conditions.

 - Stakeholder coordination: coordinate with local planning and building departments to bring all plans and drawings to full approval and compliance.

 - Construction support: support projects through RFIs, submittals, field issue resolution.

 - Product feedback: aggregate recommendations for Product Design team based on project-specific learnings.

 

Qualifications

 - Relevant training in Architecture and Urban Design.

 - Minimum 6-10 years of professional experience in architecture.

 - Proving ability of site planning abilities, zoning and code analysis.

 - Strong knowledge of IBC/IRC.

 - Proven track record of producing drawings through every stage of permitting and construction.

 - Proven ability of coordination with local jurisdictions and delivering fully compliant and approved designs.

 - Proven ability of taking designs through construction administration.

 - Ability to produce visualizations to illustrate new project feasibility and coordination (not for final project marketing and sale).

 - Excellent communication and cross-functional collaboration skills; comfortable presenting to senior stakeholders.

 - Proficiency in design software (e.g., Revit, Enscape, etc).

 - Experience working with prefabricated / modular / panelized construction is preferred but not required.

 

Additional Information

 - Candidates must be based in or ready to relocate to Austin, TX full-time.

 - The company provides comprehensive benefits, PTO, and participation in company equity programs.

The American Housing Corporation is on a mission to solve the America’s housing crisis through all-out production. We believe that America’s affordability crisis is fundamentally a production crisis, and that solving it requires a vertically integrated development approach: We buy land, and manufacture, install, and sell our homes directly to customers. The American Dream is built, not inherited. We're hiring the best engineers, designers, and builders to solve the hardest problem in America.


If you don't see anything posted that fits your skill set, tell us why you would like to be a part of The American Housing Corporation.

source_jobs.title: Director, Mechanical Engineering
source_jobs.description:
The American Housing Corporation is on a mission to solve the America’s housing crisis through all-out production. We believe that America’s affordability crisis is fundamentally a production crisis, and that solving it requires a vertically integrated development approach: We buy land, and manufacture, install, and sell our homes directly to customers. This is the only way to build at the scale necessary to solve this crisis.



The American Dream is built, not inherited. We're hiring the best engineers, designers, and builders to solve the hardest problem in America.



As Director of Mechanical Engineering, you will play a key role in the design and manufacturing of our build system — a proprietary kit-of-parts for building high-quality multifamily housing.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Lead design of both structural and non-structural build system components

 - Build and lead engineering team in charge of build component design

 - Full ownership of CAD model of components

 - Obtain and maintain all necessary certifications and approvals for build system components

 - Create engineering drawings and work instructions for build system component manufacturing and assembly

 - Design and implement production equipment, processes, and tooling

 - Track KPIs to understand how our engineering and manufacturing decisions impact the quality and $/sq ft of our buildings
   
   


QUALIFICATIONS

 - 7+ years of experience in hardware development

 - Strong technical background in mechanical design, structural analysis, DFMA, and production

 - Experience with CAD and FEA software packages

 - Proven experience delivering large complex hardware products from low-rate prototypes to full-rate production

 - Ability to work collaboratively across multidisciplinary teams in a fast-paced, dynamic environment

 - Excellent problem-solving, organizational, and leadership skills

 - Bachelor of Science or higher degree in a technical discipline preferred, not required
   
   


COMPENSATION

The overall cash compensation for this position ranges from $180,000 to $250,000. All of our positions also offer a compensation package that includes equity and robust benefits.

source_jobs.title: Lead Design Architect
source_jobs.description:
The Role: Lead Design Architect

As The Lead Design Architect, you will define the look and feel of the new generation of the American Rowhome. You will drive the design from concept to construction. You will lead the design of a new residential catalogue, bespoke style across every scale: from the facades to every corner of our homes. The role would require both creativity and meticulous attention to detail.

The role is based full-time in Austin, TX and would be ideal for an experienced designer who prefers to work hands-on, with a potential to grow a team over time.



About The Company

The American Housing Corporation http://americanhousing.co is on a mission to solve the America’s housing crisis through all-out housing production. We believe that America’s affordability crisis is fundamentally a production crisis, and that solving it requires a vertically integrated development approach: We buy land, design and manufacture, install, and sell our homes directly to customers — all by ourselves.

The American Dream is built, not inherited. We're hiring the best engineers, designers, and builders to solve the hardest problem in America. We’re based in Austin, TX and build across the country.



What You’ll Be Working On

 - Lead architectural exploration of our rowhome product catalogue, including new interior layouts, facades, and styles.

 - Lead the curation of interior and exterior finishes, fixtures, and appliances across our product lineup.

 - Partner with the Product Engineering and Supply Chain teams to identify suppliers capable of delivering intended quality at competitive price points and reliable timelines.

 - Partner with Product and Brand team on defining the unique architectural identity of our products.

 - Art direct visualizations.

 - Assist with value engineering in protecting a holistic design vision.

 - Oversee projects through construction and iterate the designs based on learnings from realized projects.



Qualifications

 - Relevant training in Architecture, Interior Design.

 - Minimum 10 years of professional experience in architecture, or industrial design.

 - Proven track record of taking designs from concept to construction.

 - Proven track record of managing material selection and finish specifications across complex projects.

 - Strong working knowledge of construction methods, trade coordination, and traditional building practices. Familiarity with modular or off-site construction methods is preferable.

 - Excellent communication and cross-functional collaboration skills; comfortable presenting to senior stakeholders.

 - Proficiency in design software (e.g., Rhino, Revit, Enscape, SolidWorks, AutoCAD, etc).

 - Experience in value engineering or cost optimization in a design-driven context.



Additional Information

 - Candidates must be based in or ready to relocate to Austin, TX full-time.

 - The company provides comprehensive benefits, PTO, and participation in company equity programs.

source_jobs.title: Director, Real Estate Development
source_jobs.description:
The American Housing Corporation is on a mission to solve America’s housing crisis through all-out production, starting with the development of rowhomes. We believe that America’s affordability crisis is fundamentally a production crisis, and that solving it requires a vertically integrated development approach: We buy land, and manufacture, install, and sell our homes directly to customers.




THE ROLE

We are hiring a Director of Real Estate Development to lead the execution of the company’s real estate platform.

This role will own the operational backbone of the real estate side of the company. Driving underwriting, development, and deal execution across markets. You will be building the real estate operating structure that will allow American Housing to deploy capital and build housing at national scale.

You will work directly with CEO Riley Meik to translate company strategy into a scalable development pipeline. You will also partner closely with co-founder Bobby Fijan, who leads investor relationships, political engagement, and high-level real estate strategy.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

Development Strategy

 - Work with leadership to determine market entry strategies and capital deployment priorities

 - Establish internal processes for pipeline management, underwriting, and deal tracking

 - Evaluate development opportunities that align with AHC’s manufacturing-based housing system

 - Ensure the development pipeline supports factory production capacity

Underwriting, Investment Analysis, Financing

 - Develop standardized underwriting frameworks for rapid evaluation of opportunities

 - Build institutional-quality financial models for residential ground up development and BTR

 - Prepare investment materials and decision materials for leadership, investors, and lenders

Acquisitions & Deal Execution

 - Support Head of Real Estate in advancing land acquisition opportunities and vetting inbound requests

 - Lead underwriting, LOIs, due diligence, and closing processes

 - Coordinate with brokers, landowners, lenders, and external partners

 - In collaboration with Head of Real Estate and CFO secure required deal investment and financing

 - Manage multiple active deals simultaneously across markets

Development Management

 - Oversee entitlement and permitting strategy for development sites

 - Coordinate with land use attorneys, consultants, and municipalities for timeline

 - Coordinate with in-house design, manufacturing, and construction management teams on establishing robust product criteria and project delivery timelines




QUALIFICATIONS

We are looking for someone who combines institutional analytical training with real estate development execution.

Requirements:

 - 7+ years of experience in real estate development, investment, or acquisitions

 - Exceptional financial modeling and investment analysis skills

 - Experience underwriting and executing complex development deals

 - Strong understanding of land acquisition, entitlements, and development pipelines

 - Ability to operate autonomously in high-ownership environments

 - Experience managing teams or building new organizational functions

 - Comfort making decisions under uncertainty and moving quickly

Ideal candidates will also have:

 - Experience in high-growth or entrepreneurial environments

 - Strong market intuition around housing supply and urban development

 - Ability to translate strategy into executable operational plans




WHAT MAKES THIS ROLE DIFFERENT

Most real estate roles operate inside the traditional development model. This role helps build a new one.

AHC is creating a vertically integrated housing company where development, manufacturing, and construction operate as one system. Real estate decisions directly affect factory production, logistics, and installation. The Director of Real Estate Development sits at the center of that system.

You will help design the development platform that powers one of the most ambitious housing co

source_jobs.title: Lead Mechanical Engineer, Building Shell
source_jobs.description:
The American Housing Corporation is on a mission to solve the America’s housing crisis through all-out production. We believe that America’s affordability crisis is fundamentally a production crisis, and that solving it requires a vertically integrated development approach: We buy land, and manufacture, install, and sell our homes directly to customers. This is the only way to build at the scale necessary to solve this crisis.

The American Dream is built, not inherited. We're hiring the best engineers, designers, and builders to solve the hardest problem in America.

As a Lead Mechanical Engineer, you will be responsible for the design, manufacturing, and assembly of the chassis of our buildings — the floor, wall, and roof panels.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Own the design of floor, wall, and roof panels, along with all associated connectors and hardware

 - Own the design, buildout, and commissioning of the panel shop in our new factory

 - Own all improvements to the panel shop in our current factory

 - Own the packing, shipping, and rigging methods and hardware for building shell

 - Own the install methods and associated hardware for building shell onsite assembly

 - Build and lead a team of engineers, manufacturing specialists, and R&D technicians to design every component and their manufacturing and assembly methods

 - Collaborate with structural engineers, architects, and designers to make sure every component is code compliant and meets our customers needs

 - Obtain all necessary certifications and approvals for structural components

 - Manage supply chain, quality, assembly, and delivery — work with company leadership to hire for these roles in the near future

 - Establish and track KPIs to understand how our engineering and manufacturing decisions are impacting the quality and cost of our buildings




QUALIFICATIONS

 - 6+ years of experience in hardware development

 - Strong technical background in mechanical design, structural analysis, DFMA, and production

 - Proven experience delivering large complex hardware products from low-rate prototypes to full-rate production

 - Ability to work collaboratively across multidisciplinary teams in a fast-paced, dynamic environment

 - Excellent problem-solving, organizational, and leadership skills

 - Extremely experienced with CAD/CAM/PLM, part families, and parametric modeling — experience with Siemens NX and Teamcenter preferred

 - Bachelor of Science or higher degree in a technical discipline preferred, not required


COMPENSATION

The overall cash compensation for this position ranges from $160,000 to $200,000. All of our positions also offer a compensation package that includes equity and robust health benefits.

source_jobs.title: Design Technologist / BIM Lead - Product Platform
source_jobs.description:
ROLE SUMMARY

The American Housing Corporation (AHC) is developing a prefabricated, panelized residential product platform for rowhome and low-rise multifamily applications. Design Technologist / BIM Lead will own the digital production flow and design automation on the Product team. You will implement BIM-management best-practices, experiment with cutting edge AI design automations, maintain the source-of-truth models for all of our building components. This role is central to creating a seamless workflow between product development and scalable project delivery.

You will execute across the full stack: design, modeling, documentation, factory, and the field. You think in systems and platforms, move quickly and iteratively, and are comfortable living in the design to manufacturing to construction loop. You take ownership past the traditional architectural boundary, and you're organized and quality-control minded.



ABOUT THE COMPANY

The American Housing Corporation http://americanhousing.co/ is on a mission to solve the America’s housing crisis through all-out housing production. We believe that America’s affordability crisis is fundamentally a production crisis, and that solving it requires a vertically integrated development approach: We buy land, design and manufacture, install, and sell our homes directly to customers — all by ourselves.

The American Dream is built, not inherited. We're hiring the best engineers, designers, and builders to solve the hardest problem in America. We’re based in Austin, TX and build across the country.




CORE RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Design automation strategy. In collaboration with Product and Software teams decide how the product platform and its workflows scale. Propose ways to expedite product development and scale project delivery across multiple jurisdictions. .

 - The product platform. Build and maintain a BIM library of kit-of-parts and rules-based configuration system governing unit layouts, stacking, and variation — all operating within structural, MEP, manufacturing, and code constraints.

 - Staged productization, tied to certification. Advance each component through concept to BIM to construction documentation to field validation.

 - BOM integration. Work with Software, Manufacturing, and Construction teams on automating BOM generation from concept feasibility to construction documentation.

 - Distribution and handoff. Define how the system is communicated and handed off to manufacturing, the field, jurisdiction authorities, and future projects — panelization logic, fabrication-intent details, install sequencing, and the standards that let the next project move fast.

 - Integration and documentation control. Act as the connective point across Architecture, Structural, MEP, manufacturing, software, and in-field teams, resolving conflicts in the model rather than the field. Maintain fabrication-aligned BIM models, permit-ready drawing sets, and a controlled component library consistent across every product variation. Produce high-quality drawings for all components. Automate parts of drawing production using the BIM library of parts.


REQUIRED EXPERIENCE

 - Advanced digital workflow / cross-platform fluency. Required: advanced modeling in Revit, ACC, Rhino, Dynamo, Grasshopper, and visualization software. Proven ability to move data across software and platforms and to design how information flows through a toolchain.

 - Agentic / AI-assisted tool development is a plus.

 - Licensed architect (or on a clear path to licensure).

 - 6–10+ years in architecture, with hands-on experience in building-system coordination, modeling, documentation, and construction.

 - Strong grasp of architectural documentation fundamentals — what gets documented, and to whom — alongside building-systems integration and CD standards.

 - Proven ability to maintain complex systems across multiple product and project iterations.

 - Prefabrication / modular / panelized construction

 - Product-plat

source_jobs.title: Construction Manager, Factory Buildout
source_jobs.description:
The American Housing Corporation is on a mission to solve America’s housing crisis through all-out production. We believe that America’s affordability crisis is fundamentally a production crisis, and that solving it requires a vertically integrated development approach: We buy land, and manufacture, install, and sell our homes directly to customers. This is the only way to build at the scale necessary to solve this crisis.

The American Dream is built, not inherited. We’re hiring the best engineers, designers, and builders to solve the hardest problem in America.

As Construction Manager, you will own the buildout of our new factory — the physical foundation of everything we make. You will manage our general contractor, coordinate our in-house architecture and engineering team, and drive the project from preconstruction and permitting through construction, commissioning, and handover — delivering a safe, on-budget, on-schedule facility built to scale into full-rate production.



WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Own delivery of our new factory buildout — manage the general contractor and coordinate our in-house architecture and engineering team, from preconstruction through commissioning

 - Own construction coordination and installation of all base building and process systems, built to the in-house design: HVAC, electrical, plumbing, compressed air, dust collection, life safety, and process utilities

 - Coordinate permitting and inspections with the in-house design team and authorities having jurisdiction — keeping approvals off the critical path

 - Execute the facility layout in partnership with manufacturing engineering to support material flow, line balance, and future expansion

 - Own the construction schedule — track critical path, manage milestones, and hold the GC and vendors accountable to the plan

 - Own the project budget — track costs, review the GC's buyout and change orders, and identify savings without compromising schedule or safety

 - Hold the GC accountable for site safety and code compliance — OSHA, NFPA, EPA, fire marshal, and local inspections

 - Establish and track KPIs for schedule adherence, budget variance, and construction quality

 - Drive commissioning, punch list closeout, and turnover of all building and process systems to operations


QUALIFICATIONS

 - 7+ years of experience in construction management or project management — ideally in a manufacturing, industrial, or heavy production environment

 - Direct experience leading a factory or industrial buildout from greenfield or fit-out through commissioning and ramp

 - Proven track record managing a general contractor and vendors — including contract administration, change-order management, and schedule control

 - Strong working knowledge of building and process systems: HVAC, electrical, plumbing, structural, life safety, and process utilities — with sharp constructability judgment

 - Familiarity with building codes, OSHA standards, NFPA, and local permitting processes

 - Proficient with construction management software and scheduling tools; CAD or facility-planning tools a plus

 - Proven track record managing multi-million dollar capital construction budgets

 - Ability to work collaboratively across multidisciplinary teams in a fast-paced, dynamic environment

 - Excellent problem-solving, organizational, and leadership skills

 - Bachelor's degree in engineering, construction management, or a related technical discipline preferred, not required

 - PE, PMP, CCM, or LEED certification a plus


ADDITIONAL INFORMATION

 - Candidates must be based in or ready to relocate to Austin, TX full-time.

 - As AHC scales, this role has the opportunity to expand into broader construction management roles across the company

 - AHC provides comprehensive benefits, PTO, and participation in company equity programs.

source_jobs.title: Senior Software Engineer
source_jobs.description:
As a Senior Software Engineer, you will help build Alamo, the internal MES/ERP platform powering The American Housing Corporation’s design, real estate, manufacturing, operations, and supply chain workflows.

You will work across the product surface: from polished user-facing workflows in Next.js to durable backend services, database models, integrations, and operational tooling. You will build software that helps architects design and configure homes, helps real estate teams identify developable opportunities, and helps manufacturing teams turn designs into physical products.

This role is ideal for an experienced engineer who likes working close to the business, understands that excellent software is both technically rigorous and deeply practical, and wants to help build the operating system for a new kind of housing company.



What You’ll Be Working On

 - Build fullstack product workflows for Alamo, our internal MES/ERP system.

 - Build software that helps architects design, configure, and refine homes from concept through production.

 - Build tools that help the real estate team evaluate land, identify developable opportunities, and understand project feasibility.

 - Design and implement production-grade frontend experiences using Next.js, TypeScript, Tailwind, and shadcn/ui.

 - Build backend services and APIs using NestJS, TypeScript, PostgreSQL, Drizzle ORM, and shared Zod contracts.

 - Model complex operational domains including parts, inventory, production, purchasing, quality, work orders, real estate, design systems, and factory workflows.

 - Partner directly with manufacturing, supply chain, finance, design, real estate, and operations teams to turn messy real-world processes into clear, reliable software.

 - Own features from discovery through implementation, rollout, iteration, and long-term maintenance.

 - Help establish engineering patterns for a system that will scale from early internal tooling into mission-critical infrastructure.

 - Improve system reliability, observability, performance, security, and developer experience.



Qualifications

 - 6+ years of professional software engineering experience.

 - Strong experience building fullstack web applications in TypeScript.

 - Deep familiarity with React and modern frontend architecture.

 - Strong backend engineering fundamentals, including API design, data modeling, service boundaries, and production debugging.

 - Experience with relational databases, especially PostgreSQL.

 - Track record of shipping complex product features in ambiguous environments.

 - Strong product judgment and ability to work closely with non-technical operators and stakeholders.

 - Care for craft: clean abstractions, thoughtful UI, robust tests, and maintainable systems.

 - Comfortable working in a fast-moving, high-ownership environment.

 - Experience with manufacturing, ERP, MES, inventory, logistics, construction, or industrial software is a plus.
   
   

Additional Information

 - The company provides comprehensive benefits, PTO, and participation in company equity programs.

 - This is an in-person role for builders who want to work closely with the people using the software every day.

 - No visa sponsorship.

source_jobs.title: Project Delivery Lead
source_jobs.description:
The American Housing Corporation is on a mission to solve America's housing crisis through all-out production. We believe that America's affordability crisis is fundamentally a production crisis, and that solving it requires a vertically integrated development approach: We buy land, and manufacture, install, and sell our homes directly to customers. This is the only way to build at the scale necessary to solve this crisis.

The American Dream is built, not inherited. We're hiring the best engineers, designers, and builders to solve the hardest problem in America.

As Project Delivery Lead, you own a project from the day it enters diligence to the day it becomes a finished home that Sales accepts as ready to sell. You are engaged from the start, pressure-testing whether a deal can actually be delivered before we commit to it, then turning our land team's feasibility and our project architects' drawings into a permitted, priced, scheduled, build-ready plan and carrying it through production, install, and the field as the single owner of schedule, budget, and risk. You are the connective tissue that keeps nothing from falling in the seams between teams, and you help build the process and system of record that tell the company how every project is really performing. You will be measured above all on delivering projects on time, on budget, and at high quality, with no surprises. This is a high-ownership, high-visibility role reporting directly to the CEO, with a clear path to grow as you prove you can carry projects at volume.

WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Engage from diligence. Partner with the land team and underwriting to pressure-test constructability, cost, and schedule before a deal is committed, so we only take on projects we can deliver

 - Own each project end to end as the single point of accountability, from diligence through design, production, install, and field construction, to a home accepted by Sales as ready for sale

 - Own the schedule. Build and maintain the critical path across design, factory, install, and field, and drive momentum through every handoff

 - Own the budget. Manage each project to its approved cost, track cost-to-complete, protect margin, and release spend only when scope is complete and quality standards are met

 - Own risk. Spot problems early, size them, and drive mitigation before they compound, holding the line on the standard product rather than absorbing scope creep

 - Help build the process and the system of record for performance. Stand up the stage gates, handoff standards, and reporting that track schedule, cost, quality, and risk, and be the single source of truth for project performance

 - Coordinate across real estate operations, project architects, production, install, field operations, and sales. Align factory output and site readiness so the whole chain moves as one, and own both handoffs: a clean brief in from the land team, a documented, ready-for-sale home out to Sales

 - Drive quality and drive down punch and rework, holding every stage to scope and standard through checklists, gates, and field verification

 - Give leadership a clear, honest read. Run project reviews, keep documentation current, and communicate changes to scope, plan, and spec proactively

QUALIFICATIONS

 - 8+ years delivering complex residential or construction projects, with direct budget-and-schedule ownership across multiple functions (production homebuilding, manufactured or modular housing, or vertically integrated development strongly preferred)

 - A track record of owning projects end to end and delivering them on time, on budget, and to standard, not just executing one phase

 - Experience building process and structure where little existed, standing up the gates, playbooks, and reporting that let a team deliver consistently at volume

 - Comfortable with metrics and reporting. You help build the system of record, track performance rigorously, and use data to drive decisions rather than react to them

source_jobs.title: Product Engineer
source_jobs.description:
As a Senior Product Engineer, you will help build Alamo, the internal software platform that powers The American Housing Corporation’s manufacturing, supply chain, inventory, production, real estate, design, and operations workflows.

You will work directly with the people running the business to understand real operational problems, shape product solutions, and ship software that makes the company faster, clearer, and more capable. You will build tools that help architects design homes, help real estate teams identify developable opportunities, and help manufacturing teams turn those designs into physical products.

This role is ideal for an engineer with strong product instincts, excellent taste in user experience, and the technical depth to take ideas from ambiguous problem to reliable production system.

The role is based full-time in Austin, TX.



What You’ll Be Working On

 - Build product workflows for Alamo, our internal MES/ERP platform.

 - Build software that helps architects design, configure, and refine homes from concept through production.

 - Build tools that help the real estate team evaluate land, identify developable opportunities, and understand project feasibility.

 - Work closely with manufacturing, supply chain, finance, design, real estate, and operations teams to understand their workflows and identify high-leverage product opportunities.

 - Turn messy operational processes into simple, elegant, and reliable software.

 - Own features end-to-end: discovery, design, implementation, rollout, iteration, and maintenance.

 - Build polished user experiences using Next.js, TypeScript, Tailwind, and shadcn/ui.

 - Develop backend services, database models, and APIs that support mission-critical operational workflows.

 - Help define the product and design standards for internal software at AHC.

 - Improve speed, quality, traceability, and decision-making across the business.



Qualifications

 - 5+ years of professional software engineering experience.

 - Strong fullstack engineering ability, especially with TypeScript, React, and relational databases.

 - Excellent product judgment and a track record of building software that users genuinely adopt.

 - Strong taste in user experience, information architecture, and workflow design.

 - Ability to work directly with operators, understand their problems, and translate them into clear product requirements.

 - Comfort operating in ambiguity and making thoughtful tradeoffs.

 - Strong communication skills and a high degree of ownership.

 - Experience building internal tools, ERP, MES, inventory, logistics, manufacturing, construction, or operational software is a plus.



Additional Information

 - The company provides comprehensive benefits, PTO, and participation in company equity programs.

 - This is an in-person role for builders who want to work closely with the people using the software every day.

 - No visa sponsorship.

notes: Vertically integrated development approach, focus on solving America's housing crisis through production
